title: Multi-agent infrastructure — registry, mailbox, fork, monitor, goals
purpose: Build the multi-agent infrastructure that all agent coordination depends on. AgentRegistry tracks active agents with hierarchical paths and no concurrency limits. Mailbox provides inter-agent messaging with sequence numbers. Fork logic inherits filtered context and returns structured audit results. RunMonitored delegates background task watching to a lightweight model. Goal tracking manages objectives, budgets, and continuation policies. This is infrastructure — the tools that wrap it (SpawnAgent, Fork tool, etc.) are in N-015.
task: Implement the five agent/ modules: registry.rs (AgentRegistry with two-phase spawn, RAII cleanup, hierarchical paths, no concurrency cap), mailbox.rs (Mailbox with trigger_turn flag and sequence numbers for wait-for-any), fork.rs (fork logic with filtered context inheritance, model switching, structured ForkAudit result), monitor.rs (RunMonitored with lightweight model delegation and query handle), goals.rs (GoalTracker with objectives, token/time budgets, continuation policies). Testing: Layer 1 unit tests for each module.

requirements.title: AgentRegistry with hierarchical paths and statuses
requirements.spec: AgentRegistry SHALL track active agents by path (hierarchical, e.g. /workflow/dev/agent-1) with statuses (Spawning, Active, Completing, Completed, Failed). No hardcoded concurrency limits.
acceptance: AgentRegistry struct in crates/norn/src/agent/registry.rs
AgentEntry struct: id (Uuid), path (String), role (String), status (AgentStatus), model (String), spawned_at (DateTime<Utc>), parent_id (Option<Uuid>)
AgentStatus enum: Spawning, Active, Completing, Completed, Failed
get(id) returns Option<&AgentEntry>
get_by_path(path) returns Option<&AgentEntry>
list() returns iterator over all entries
children(parent_id) returns iterator over direct children
No field or constant caps agent count (C66)
Test: register 100 agents, verify all accessible

requirements.title: Two-phase spawn reservation with RAII cleanup
requirements.spec: Spawning an agent SHALL be two-phase: reserve (allocates ID, sets Spawning status, returns SpawnGuard) then confirm (sets Active). If the SpawnGuard drops without confirm, the entry is removed.
acceptance: reserve(path, role, model, parent_id) returns Result<SpawnGuard>
SpawnGuard holds Arc<parking_lot::RwLock<AgentRegistry>> and agent ID
SpawnGuard::confirm(self) transitions status to Active and consumes the guard
Drop impl on SpawnGuard removes the entry if not confirmed
Duplicate path during reserve returns error
Test: reserve without confirm — verify entry cleaned up on drop
Test: reserve then confirm — verify entry persists with Active status

requirements.title: Mailbox with trigger_turn and sequence numbers
requirements.spec: Mailbox SHALL support inter-agent messaging with a trigger_turn flag for immediate vs deferred delivery. Sequence numbers on each mailbox SHALL enable efficient wait-for-any without polling.
acceptance: Mailbox struct in crates/norn/src/agent/mailbox.rs
MailboxMessage struct: id (Uuid), from (Uuid), to (Uuid), content (serde_json::Value), trigger_turn (bool), sequence (u64), timestamp (DateTime<Utc>)
send(from, to, content, trigger_turn) appends message and increments sequence
recv(agent_id) returns Vec<MailboxMessage> draining pending messages
peek(agent_id) returns count of pending messages without draining
wait_for_any(agent_ids, since_sequences) returns when any mailbox has new messages past the given sequence numbers — uses tokio::sync::Notify
Test: send 3 messages, recv drains all 3, second recv returns empty
Test: wait_for_any wakes when message arrives

requirements.title: Fork logic with filtered context and audit result
requirements.spec: Fork SHALL create a child agent that inherits filtered parent context (selected events, suppressed tool calls), executes on a potentially different model, and returns a structured ForkAudit to the parent.
acceptance: ForkConfig struct: task (String), model (String), context_filter (ContextFilter), tools (Option<Vec<String>>)
ContextFilter struct: include_system (bool), include_recent_n (Option<usize>), exclude_tool_calls (bool)
ForkAudit struct: fork_id (Uuid), model (String), result (serde_json::Value), usage (Usage), duration (Duration), events_count (usize)
fork(parent_session, config, provider, registry) returns Result<ForkAudit>
Fork registers as a child in the AgentRegistry with parent_id set
Fork entry cleaned up (Completed/Failed) when fork returns
Test: fork with context filter that excludes tool calls, verify filtered events

requirements.title: RunMonitored with lightweight model delegation
requirements.spec: RunMonitored SHALL delegate monitoring of a long-running task to a lightweight model. The parent receives a MonitorHandle for querying progress without consuming full output. — SUPERSEDED 2026-07-03 (owner ruling): the RunMonitored scaffolding this requirement produced was deleted (agent/monitor.rs no longer exists); do NOT re-dispatch this requirement. Forward design: watcher internal agents over the managed background-process manager, docs/design/norn/INTERNAL-AGENTS.md (see docs/DECISIONS-2026-07.md §4).
acceptance: MonitorConfig struct: task_description (String), monitor_model (String), poll_interval (Duration)
MonitorHandle struct wraps a tokio::sync::watch::Receiver<MonitorStatus>
MonitorStatus struct: summary (String), progress_pct (Option<f32>), is_complete (bool), last_updated (DateTime<Utc>)
MonitorHandle::query() returns current MonitorStatus
MonitorHandle::wait_complete() awaits is_complete == true
run_monitored(config, task_future, provider) returns (JoinHandle<TaskResult>, MonitorHandle)
Test: run_monitored on a mock task, verify MonitorHandle returns status updates

requirements.title: Goal tracking with budgets and continuation
requirements.spec: GoalTracker SHALL manage objectives, token budgets, time budgets, and continuation policies. When a budget threshold is reached, it signals the appropriate continuation action.
acceptance: GoalTracker struct in crates/norn/src/agent/goals.rs
Goal struct: description (String), token_budget (Option<u64>), time_budget (Option<Duration>), continuation (ContinuationPolicy)
ContinuationPolicy enum: Stop, Handoff { summary_prompt: String }, Continue { recapitulate: bool }
GoalTracker::check(usage, elapsed) returns GoalSignal
GoalSignal enum: OnTrack, BudgetWarning { pct_used: f32 }, BudgetExceeded { policy: ContinuationPolicy }
BudgetWarning triggers at configurable threshold (default 80%)
Test: set token budget 1000, report 800 used, verify BudgetWarning
Test: set token budget 1000, report 1100 used, verify BudgetExceeded with correct policy

requirements.title: Session-dispatched cron scheduling
requirements.spec: ScheduleEntry SHALL define cron-like schedules that re-launch agent sessions without keeping them alive between runs. The scheduler stores entries; execution is handled by the integration layer.
acceptance: ScheduleEntry struct: id (Uuid), cron_expr (String), agent_config (serde_json::Value), enabled (bool), last_run (Option<DateTime<Utc>>), next_run (Option<DateTime<Utc>>)
Scheduler struct stores entries in a HashMap<Uuid, ScheduleEntry>
Scheduler::add(entry) registers a schedule
Scheduler::remove(id) removes a schedule
Scheduler::due_entries(now) returns entries where next_run <= now and enabled
Scheduler::advance(id, now) updates last_run and computes next_run from cron_expr
Cargo.toml adds cron crate for expression parsing
Test: add entry with '*/5 * * * *', verify due_entries fires at correct interval

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement agent tools (SpawnAgent, Fork tool, SendMessage, etc.) — those are N-015
SHALL NOT implement the agent loop — only the infrastructure it uses
SHALL NOT implement provider selection or model routing — fork uses a provider passed by the caller
SHALL NOT implement session persistence to disk — registry and mailbox are in-memory
SHALL NOT implement actual cron execution — only the schedule store and due-entry queries
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: cargo test -p norn — all agent module tests pass
No file over 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
agent/mod.rs contains only pub mod declarations and pub use re-exports
grep -rn 'unwrap()\|expect(' crates/norn/src/agent/ returns no matches outside test code
